patch.value: GH-599: git merge driver + documented workflow for append-only history/*.jsonl (concurrent appends fork the hash chain)

Union-style git merge driver (or documented gitattributes strategy) ships for history/*.jsonl preserving hash-chain repairability; concurrent same-item appends on two branches merge without manual conflict resolution; post-merge chain state is either valid or flagged by validate/health with a history-repair hint; workflow documented

GH-599. history/<id>.jsonl is append-only + hash-chained — ideal for conflict-free merging — but no merge driver/gitattributes ships, so concurrent same-item appends on two branches produce a content conflict; naive union leaves a forked hash chain needing manual history-repair. Fix direction: ship a union merge driver that re-anchors the chain deterministically (or documents gitattributes union + mandatory post-merge history-repair), plus post-merge detection wiring per pm-g5sx AC. Coordinate with pm-gpo7 (repair must not silently discard) and pm-ol3p (verify gate).
